heading: Bucket Results through Facet Queries
body:
The following query uses the [$searchMeta](pipe.$searchMeta) stage to return
unique values of movies that contain the term `Gravity` in the
`title` field of the `movies` collection. The index definition of
this query limits the results returned from a
[token](bson-data-types-token) index, where both the
[autocomplete](autocomplete-ref) and [token](bson-data-types-token) types are on the `title` field.

```kotlin
import com.mongodb.client.model.Aggregates.limit
import com.mongodb.client.model.Aggregates.project
import com.mongodb.client.model.Filters.eq
import com.mongodb.client.model.Projections.*
import com.mongodb.kotlin.client.coroutine.MongoClient
import kotlinx.coroutines.runBlocking
import org.bson.Document

fun main() {
    val uri = "<connection-string>"
    val mongoClient = MongoClient.create(uri)
    val database = mongoClient.getDatabase("sample_mflix")
    val collection = database.getCollection<Document>("movies")

    runBlocking {
        val agg = Document(
            "\$searchMeta", Document(
                "facet",
                Document(
                    "operator",
                    Document(
                        "autocomplete",
                        Document("path", "title")
                            .append("query", "Gravity")
                    )
                )
                    .append(
                        "facets",
                        Document(
                            "titleFacet",
                            Document("type", "string").append("path", "title")
                        )
                    )
            )
        )

        val resultsFlow = collection.aggregate<Document>(listOf(agg))
        resultsFlow.collect { println(it) }
    }
    mongoClient.close()
}
```

```none
Document{{count=Document{{lowerBound=5}},
facet=Document{{titleFacet=Document{{buckets=[Document{{_id=Gravity,
count=3}}, Document{{_id=Defying Gravity, count=1}}, Document{{_id=Laws
of Gravity, count=1}}]}}}}}}
```

MongoDB Search returns documents that contain the term `Gravity` in the
`title` field. The `count` field in the results indicate the number
of documents in the collection with the same title. In the results,
MongoDB Search found three documents in the collection with `Gravity` as its
title, but MongoDB Search omitted the duplicate titles and returned only one
matching document.

If you want to see the duplicate titles as shown in the results below,
run the preceding [autocomplete](autocomplete-ref) operator query
without [faceted navigation](fts-facet-ref) and use
the [$search](pipe.$search) stage instead. You must also use the
[$project](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/project) stage to exclude all fields except `title`.

```none
Document{{title=Gravity}}
Document{{title=Gravity}}
Document{{title=Gravity}}
Document{{title=Defying Gravity}}
Document{{title=Laws of Gravity}}
```

codeBlocks.content:
import com.mongodb.client.model.Aggregates.limit
import com.mongodb.client.model.Aggregates.project
import com.mongodb.client.model.Filters.eq
import com.mongodb.client.model.Projections.*
import com.mongodb.kotlin.client.coroutine.MongoClient
import kotlinx.coroutines.runBlocking
import org.bson.Document

fun main() {
    val uri = "<connection-string>"
    val mongoClient = MongoClient.create(uri)
    val database = mongoClient.getDatabase("sample_mflix")
    val collection = database.getCollection<Document>("movies")

    runBlocking {
        val agg = Document(
            "\$searchMeta", Document(
                "facet",
                Document(
                    "operator",
                    Document(
                        "autocomplete",
                        Document("path", "title")
                            .append("query", "Gravity")
                    )
                )
                    .append(
                        "facets",
                        Document(
                            "titleFacet",
                            Document("type", "string").append("path", "title")
                        )
                    )
            )
        )

        val resultsFlow = collection.aggregate<Document>(listOf(agg))
        resultsFlow.collect { println(it) }
    }
    mongoClient.close()
}

Document{{count=Document{{lowerBound=5}},
facet=Document{{titleFacet=Document{{buckets=[Document{{_id=Gravity,
count=3}}, Document{{_id=Defying Gravity, count=1}}, Document{{_id=Laws
of Gravity, count=1}}]}}}}}}
